div1.entryFree: Slink (impf. slunk) to move stealthily, to creep: we will s. away in supper-time, Merch. II, 4, 1. s. by and note him, As III, 2, 267. or slunk not Saturnine, as Tarquin erst, that left the camp to sin in Lucrece' bed? Tit. IV, 1, 63. his familiars to his buried fortunes s. all away, Tim. IV, 2, 11.
